title: habenaria macroceratitis
intro_study: According to "Plants (Basel, Switzerland)", habenaria macroceratitis, also known as HM-V, has anti-inflammatory benefits, particularly in inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines such as CCL17 and CCL22, which are associated with chronic inflammatory skin diseases like atopic dermatitis.
side_effects: Habenaria macroceratitis can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing nausea, dizziness, and stomach upset due to its high concentration of alkaloids. In severe cases, excessive consumption may lead to respiratory problems, including difficulty breathing and wheezing. Furthermore, improper use of the plant can cause allergic reactions, resulting in skin irritation, itching, and redness. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can also damage the kidneys and liver, leading to long-term health issues.
